title: Fix --pull=true||false and add --pull-never to bud and from
body:
Prior to this fix, if someone did `buildah bud --pull=false .` and the image in
the Containerfile's FROM statement was not local, the build would fail. The same
build on Docker will succeed. In Docker, when `--pull` is set to false, it only
pulls the image from the registry if there was not one locally. Buildah would never
pull the image and if the image was not locally available, it would throw an error.
In certain Kubernetes environments, this was especially troublesome.

To retain the old `--pull=false` functionality, I've created a new `--pull-never`
option that fails if an image is not locally available just like the old
`--pull=false` option used to do.

In addition, if there was a newer version of the image on the repository than 
the one locally, the `--pull=true` option would not pull the image as it should 
have, this corrects that.

Changes both the from and bud commands.


Addresses: #1675

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body: FYI: @mheon

All green test buttons.  Could use some LGTM's or comments on things to change.  This does change the `--pull=false` current functionality for Buildah and then Podman once merged, that I think is the main concern here.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably e2c33f3) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably f50b55d) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Green Test buttons.  @rhatdan I think I've addressed your concerns.  Can we move forward with this or is there more to do?
@saschagrunert I thought you'd recently spun up an issue about --pull not working if the image was newer on the repo?  I can't find it if you did, could you tag it here please?
@rhatdan reworked with your suggestions.  I've also corrected `--pull=true` to pull if there's no local image or if the image on the repo is newer than the one that's stored locally.

> @saschagrunert I thought you'd recently spun up an issue about --pull not working if the image was newer on the repo? I can't find it if you did, could you tag it here please?

Yes, it was this one: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4252
@saschagrunert PTAL at this PR and see if it fixes your issues?
What is the difference between --pull=false and --pull-never, I would think they are the same thing, and that --pull-never would not be necessary.

`--pull=false` does a pull from the repo only if the image isn't stored locally.   `--pull-never` will never pull from the registry, it will either use the locally stored image or it will fail.  

I think --pull-never might be valuable in some small cases and as that was the prior functionality for `--pull=false`, I added that option in case someone still wanted that old `--pull=false` functionality to be available.
LGTM, if this matches Docker functionality, I am fine with it.
@nalind @giuseppe @vrothberg @saschagrunert @QiWang19 PTAL
LGTM
